name: Cape Town
summary: Cape Town sits at the southern tip of Africa, watched over by flat-topped Table Mountain. It is famous for its harbour, wild African penguins on the beach, and the prison island where Nelson Mandela was once held.
funFacts: Table Mountain is so flat on top it looks like a giant table, and it often wears a fluffy white cloud that locals call 'the tablecloth'.
Around 2,000 wild African penguins live at Boulders Beach near Cape Town, and they bray like little donkeys when calling each other.
Nelson Mandela was kept prisoner on Robben Island for 18 years inside a tiny stone cell barely bigger than a bathroom.
Cape Town has the world's smallest plant kingdom right on its doorstep, with around 9,000 kinds of flower, most found nowhere else on Earth.
South Africa has 11 official languages, so on one Cape Town street you might hear Xhosa clicks, Afrikaans, Zulu and English all at once.
thingsToDo: Ride the spinning cable car up Table Mountain to peek at the city from above the clouds.
Wander the V&A Waterfront harbour to watch boats, seals and street performers.
Tiptoe along the boardwalks at Boulders Beach to meet wild African penguins.
Catch a ferry to Robben Island and step inside Nelson Mandela's tiny prison cell.
Drive to Cape of Good Hope, the dramatic cliffs at the very tip of Africa.
Walk the curvy Boomslang treetop bridge in Kirstenbosch Botanical Garden.

wildlife.name: African Penguin
wildlife.description: Black-and-white seabird that waddles on Boulders Beach and brays like a donkey.

wildlife.name: Cape Fur Seal
wildlife.description: Whiskery brown seal that splashes around the harbour and barks loudly on the rocks.

wildlife.name: Southern Right Whale
wildlife.description: Huge gentle whale that swims close to Cape Town's coast every winter to have babies.

wildlife.name: Dassie
wildlife.description: Furry guinea-pig-shaped 'rock rabbit' that suns itself on Table Mountain boulders.

localWord.meaning: Hello to one person, in Xhosa, one of South Africa's most-spoken languages.

images.alt: Flat-topped Table Mountain rises above Cape Town's city bowl and harbour at golden hour.
Boats bob in the V&A Waterfront harbour with flat-topped Table Mountain behind.
African penguins waddle across the sandy boulders at Boulders Beach.
Robben Island sits in Table Bay, seen from the slopes of Table Mountain.
Rocky cliffs and blue ocean at the Cape of Good Hope, the southern tip of Africa.
Wide view of Kirstenbosch Botanical Garden with Table Mountain rising behind.
Closeup of an African penguin showing its black-and-white feathers and pink eye patch.
images.credit: Dr. P. Dee Boersma / U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service

images.alt: A whiskery Cape fur seal rests on rocks at Hout Bay near Cape Town.
A southern right whale floats at the ocean surface with its calf resting on top.
A furry dassie (rock hyrax) sitting on a rock at Cape Point.
